Ms. Robello informed the Court that a draft of the accounting has been prepared, which in final form will be the first and sinal accounting for the trust. She said that her clients were going through their records to locate receipts for reimbursement in order to finalize the petition. She requested that the Court grant an additional continuance. The Court expressed concern that little progress has been made, but agreed to a final continuance. The matter was continued to July 25, 2017 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Transfer Of Property Back To Trust
Off Calendar Oct-30-2013 Continued To Jan-08-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kevin Lachona for and with Guadalupe Cruz and Sandra Funes. Ernest Der Court appointed Counsel for Carmen Funes.

The Court stated that it reviewed the supplemental status report filed October 23, 2013. Mr. Lachona stated that potential renters viewed the property last week, that they have not secured a renter, that they hired John Farrell to assist, that they are asking $5,900.00 a month and that they are willing to reduce the amount if the property does not get rented. Mr. Lachona also stated that the stove top was installed, that the leak was repaired, that there is about $100,000.00 left in the refinance proceeds, that they agreed upon the amount of $17,250.00 to be paid to the care home for late payments and that payments to the care home are current. Mr. Der had nothing to add. Status on transfer of the property back to trust was continued to January 8, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A status report is to be filed by January 3, 2014. The Court stated that the best way to address the remaining issue of the requests for reimbursement of $2,900.00 to Guadalupe and $6,400.00 to Sandra is to include it the next accounting.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Whitney. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-05-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sheila Robello for and with Guadalupe Cruz and Sandra Funes-Tynan
(Co-Conservators).

Ms. Robello stated that alot of permits were required to do the repairs on the property, that the Contractor estimates another three weeks, that their goal is to have the house rented by September 1, 2013 and that only the interior of the house has been repaired. Ms. Robello also stated that the retaining wall can be done after the house is rented, that increasing bond is appropriate, that she will file a Substitution of Attorney in the Conservatorship matter and that they are negotiating the interest amount with regard to the amount owed to the care home. Furthermore, Ms. Robello stated that there is $10,000.00 left to pay the Contractor, that the amount does not include the retaining wall or the issue of the foundation, and asked that she be allowed to do the previous requested reimbursement amounts of $2,945.00 to Guadalupe and $6,407.50 to Sandra. Mr. Der stated that he was fine with the accounting but that he would like to see the house rented. The accounting petition was taken under submission. Status on the property was continued to September 18, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court would like a statement as to where things stand before approving any reimbursement. The issue of reimbursement is to be addressed on September 18, 2013.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sheila Robello for and with Guadalupe Cruz and Sandra Funes-Tynan (Trustees). Ernest Der Court appointed Counsel for Carmen Funes.

Ms. Robello stated that they are still negotiating with the Care Facility with regard to the late fees, that they now have a quote of $32,000.00 (close to the amount of funds they have left) from another Contractor to complete repairs, that they are still on target with a rental date of June 2013 and that the Conservatee's needs are being met. Mr. Der stated that the Conservatee's care needs are proceeding according to plan, spoke of the Conservatee's medical condition, and stated that the facility provides a lower level of care but that he was okay with it. Status of the property was taken off calendar. Status on transfer of property back to Trust was continued to April 16, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and then will be continued to the hearing date given to the accounting petition. Ms. Robello is to inform the Clerk of the hearing date on the accounting at which time the Clerk will set status on rental of the property for that same date.

Petition For Authority To Refinance And Enter Into New Loan For In A Total Amount Of $400,000 Secured By Trust Property Located At 4024 25th St., Sf, Ca: And For Reimbursement To Co-Trustees Of Costs Advanced
Off Calendar Jul-03-2012 Advanced From Aug-29-12 To Jul-17-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of The Property
Off Calendar Jul-17-2012 Continued To Sep-18-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for Carmen Funes. Sheila Robello for and with Guadalupe Cruz and Sandra Funes-Tynan.

Mr. Der stated that he was given a U.S. Bank pre-approval letter for a $400,000.00 loan, that this amount would give them a good enough cushion to last maybe three to four years and that unless something else happens that the property will need to be sold at that time. Ms. Robello stated that the loan is a 30-year fixed loan at a 3.950 interest rate, that repairs to the bathroom, plumbing and one hallway were done, that they expect the tile work in the bathroom to be completed within two weeks and that the Contractor's fees are close to budget. Mr. Der also stated that his Client is well cared for, that he would like his Client to remain at her current placement and that he is willing to take a chance on the refinance. The Court stated that Mr. Der is to remain on as Court appointed Counsel for Carmen Funes. Status on the property was continued to September 18, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for Authority to Refinance and Enter Into New Loan, etc. was taken under submission. The order is to read that the loan is not to exceed a 5% interest rate. Ms. Robello will file a declaration regarding the terms of the loan.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Santiago.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of The Property
Off Calendar Jun-12-2012 Continued To Jul-17-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sheila Robello for and with Sandra Funes-Tynan and Guadalupe Cruz. Ernest Der Court appointed Counsel for Carmen Funes.

The Court expressed concern about the unproductive asset (house), about the numerous promises of the Trustees to make the property productive, about the Care Facility being behind in payment when having been told by the Trustees that they were paying the difference for care of their Mother, how the previous loan proceeds were spent, etc. Ms. Robello provided the Court with a copy of a status report on the loan and a budget on what the assets are as well as the current care costs. Ms. Robello also stated that the loan is in progress, that there is significant deferred maintenance, that the Trustees retained a Contractor that is willing to wait for payment until finalization of the loan, and that the repairs are to be completed by June 26, 2012 and then the loan will be finalized. Furthermore, Ms. Robello stated that she received a letter from the Care Facility stating that they understand the delay, that they are willing to wait for payment ($39,000.00 since May 2011) until loan funds are received, that they have no plan to evict the Conservatee and that she will file a petition to obtain a larger loan ($365,000.00) to pay off the $101,000.00 prior loan, funds advanced by the Trustees, repair cost and the care facility. Status on the property and the loan was continued to July 17, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Robello's petition regarding a larger loan is to be set for that same date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

The Court stated that it will prepare an order appointing Counsel to represent Carmen Funes (Conservatee). Mr. Livingston stated that it is still his intent to withdraw as Counsel. The Court denied Ms. Funes-Tynan's request for more time to obtain new Counsel. Status on the property was continued to June 12, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on representation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.
